Petition For Instructions [Probate Code Section 9611]
Off Calendar Nov-18-2025 Continued To Feb-20-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Brianna K. Mannion for administrator SF Public Administrator.
John E. Boessenecker for objector W.C. Cox & Co. (via Zoom).
Jacqueline Young, Deputy Attorney General, for the California Department of Health Care Services (via CourtCall).

The matter was called for hearing on the SF Public Administrator's Petition for Instructions.

The Court stated it had reviewed the materials and noted that the parties were unable to reach a resolution in mediation. Ms. Mannion reported that DHCS wished to act as the moving party and proposed further briefing. Ms. Young stated DHCS sought the opportunity to file a response brief substantiating its claim, followed by reply briefing from the Public Administrator and W.D. Cox & Company. The Court agreed that further briefing would be necessary.

Following discussion, the Court determined that it would hold a non-evidentiary special hearing for oral argument only on February 20, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. DHCS shall file a response brief with points and authorities addressing the creditor's claim and the Public Administrator's petition by December 30, 2025. SF Public Administrator and W.D. Cox & Company shall file any reply briefs by January 14, 2026. The Court indicated that a tentative ruling may be issued prior to the hearing.

Hearing on status of mediation was taken off calendar.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Nov-18-2025 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Oct-28-2025 Continued To Apr-28-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions [Probate Code Section 9611]
Off Calendar Jul-29-2025 Continued To Nov-18-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Brianna Mannion, Esq., in person, for the San Francisco Public Administrator, Petitioner.
John Boessenecker, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for W.C. Cox & CO., Objector.

At the court's query, counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the instant petition. There is discussion regarding settlement and whether the Department of Health Care Services should be involved in those discussions. The court advises that it intends to issue a Breslin Order which will include service of the same to the Department of Health Care Services. After additional discussion, the Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than Oct-28-2025. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by Nov-04-2025. The matter is continued to Nov-18-2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204and the Parties are to return on the same date for status of mediation.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Peou. Not Reported.
